headline: Congress takes a breather as both chambers go dark for the rest of the week.

senate.lastSessionSummary: The Senate convened briefly in pro forma session on Thursday and immediately adjourned, with no substantive business conducted. The chamber will resume Monday to take up judicial confirmations, including consideration of Matthew Schwartz's nomination to the Second Circuit Court of Appeals and a confirmation vote on Arthur Roberts Jones for the Southern District of Texas.

onTheFloor.title: Consideration of Matthew A. Schwartz nomination to United States Circuit Judge for the Second Circuit
Confirmation vote on Arthur Roberts Jones nomination to United States District Judge for the Southern District of Texas

house.lastSessionSummary: The House met briefly on Thursday afternoon for a pro forma session lasting just three minutes, with no votes or substantive action taken. Speaker appointed Representative Baird as Speaker pro tempore, and the chamber introduced 39 public bills and filed six committee reports before adjourning.

onTheFloor.title: Sunshine Protection Act of 2025 - establish year-round daylight saving time
National Security, Department of State, and Related Programs Appropriations Act, 2027
Take Care of America's Veterans Act
Protecting Privacy in Purchases Act - enhance consumer privacy in online transactions

committees.topic: Hearing on H.R. 139 (Sunshine Protection Act), H.R. 8595 (State Appropriations), H.R. 9237 (Veterans), and H.R. 1181 (Privacy Protection)

committees.committee: Committee on Veterans' Affairs, Subcommittee on Technology Modernization
committees.topic: PACT Act Implementation: Modernizing VA Disability Claims Through Effective Technology
